title: Why Schema-Guided Memory Makes Agents More Reliable

cards.title: Vague Entities
cards.body: Raw text stored without schema lets the LLM invent vague entity types like 'Topic' and generic edges like 'RELATES_TO'.

cards.title: Noise & Duplicates
cards.body: Long context increases noise, duplicates facts, and lets outdated statements compete with current ones.

cards.title: Unreliable Queries
cards.body: Without structure, precise queries cannot reliably retrieve the right information.

cards.title: Pydantic Models
cards.body: Pydantic models pre-define allowed entity types and typed attributes.

cards.title: Focused Extraction
cards.body: The extraction model searches for domain-specific concepts instead of guessing a schema on the fly.

cards.title: Edge Constraints
cards.body: Edge definitions constrain which entity types may be connected, rejecting invalid relationships early.

cards.title: Entity Resolution
cards.body: Entity resolution merges different surface forms (e.g., 'OpenAI', 'Open AI') into a single node.

cards.title: Temporal Validity
cards.body: Temporal fields track when a fact is valid, distinguishing current from outdated truths.

cards.title: Graceful Updates
cards.body: New facts invalidate older ones within a validity window without silent overwriting.

pages.title: The Result: A Queryable Model of the Domain

cards.title: Precise Knowledge Graph
cards.body: A schema-guided temporal knowledge graph becomes a precise, queryable model of the domain.

cards.title: Built-in Hallucination Guard
cards.body: The schema is both a storage contract and a reasoning boundary: relationships that cannot be represented cannot be casually hallucinated.
